question: What does Ch.01 state about the plant control room, and how does Ch.05 describe the interface with RCS instrumentation?

nuscale_ch05_0012.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Leakage Detection
nuscale_ch05_0012.summary: The NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) does not use traditional components with designed leakage rates, making it impractical to distinguish between identified and unidentified leakage inside containment using current instrumentation. Any leakage into containment, regardless of source, quickly vaporizes and disperses, so all leakage is treated as unidentified until specifically located and quantified by other means; leakage detection relies on RCS inventory balance and comparison with containment evacuation system (CES) flow rates. The installed leakage detection systems are reliable, redundant, and sensitive enough to support leak-before-break analyses.

nuscale_ch01_0510.title: DSRS 11.5, Rev 0: Process and Effluent Radiological Monitoring Instrumentation and Sampling SystemsII.7 Descriptions of design features and instrumentation used in primary and secondary coolant system leakage detection
nuscale_ch01_0510.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for radiological monitoring and sampling systems, specifically addressing the design features and instrumentation used for detecting leakage in primary and secondary coolant systems. It notes that NuScale’s design generally conforms to the relevant DSRS and NUREG-0800 requirements, with some site-specific operational program responsibilities deferred to the COL applicant. Partial conformance is noted where certain regulatory aspects, such as testing and operational programs, are not addressed at this stage.

nuscale_ch01_0370.title: SRP 9.4.1, Rev 3: Control Room Area Ventilation SystemII.2 Environmental and Dynamic Effects
nuscale_ch01_0370.summary: The section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with SRP 9.4.1, Rev 3 requirements for the Control Room Area Ventilation System, specifically regarding environmental and dynamic effects. It states that NuScale meets the regulatory criteria with no exceptions or additional comments for this aspect.

nuscale_ch01_0372.title: SRP 9.4.1, Rev 3: Control Room Area Ventilation SystemII.4 Control Room
nuscale_ch01_0372.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with SRP 9.4.1, Rev 3 requirements for the Control Room Area Ventilation System. It confirms compliance with criteria for protection against natural phenomena, environmental effects, and sharing of systems, noting that up to 12 modules share the same control room. For radioactive material release control, the NuScale design uses compressed air tanks for clean air instead of emergency filtration systems during accident conditions.

nuscale_ch01_0511.title: DSRS 11.6, Rev 0: Guidance on Instrumentation and Control Design Features for Process and Effluent Radiological Monitoring, and Area Radiation and Airborne Radioactivity MonitoringII.1 Installation of instrumentation or sampling equipment
nuscale_ch01_0511.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for the installation of instrumentation and sampling equipment related to radiological monitoring. It indicates that NuScale’s design generally meets the requirements of DSRS 11.6, including monitoring of release points and radiation exposure, with some site-specific operational program responsibilities deferred to the COL applicant. Partial conformance is noted for compliance with certain post-TMI action plan items, as some aspects are outside NuScale’s scope.

nuscale_ch05_0024.title: Pressurizer
nuscale_ch05_0024.summary: The pressurizer, integrated into the upper region of the reactor vessel and separated by a baffle plate, regulates Reactor Coolant System (RCS) pressure by maintaining a saturated steam-water interface during various operating conditions. It uses heaters and spray flow to control pressure, and its design ensures that normal transients do not trigger unnecessary reactor trips or safety system actuations.

nuscale_ch01_0072.title: Combined License Information Items
nuscale_ch01_0072.summary: This section explains the interface requirements between the NuScale Power Plant certified design and the site-specific design provided in the Combined License (COL) application. It distinguishes between conceptual design information (CDI) for non-certified portions and COL information items, which are site-specific responsibilities of the COL applicant. The section also notes that COL applicants must address all COL information items and provide a list of any departures from the certified design.

nuscale_ch01_0504.title: DSRS 11.5, Rev 0: Process and Effluent Radiological Monitoring Instrumentation and Sampling SystemsII.1 Installation of Instrumentation and Monitoring Equipment and Sampling and Analyses of Normal and Potential Effluent Pathways
nuscale_ch01_0504.summary: This section reviews NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for process and effluent radiological monitoring instrumentation and sampling systems. It notes that while the design generally meets requirements, there are partial conformances due to exceptions in certain regulatory guidance areas, and some responsibilities—particularly administrative and procedural controls—are deferred to the COL (Combined License) applicant.

nuscale_ch01_0853.title: SRP Appendix 18-A, Rev 0: Crediting Manual Operator Actions in Diversity and Defense-in-Depth (D3) AnalysesC.2.B Review Criteria for Phase 2 (Preliminary Validation)
nuscale_ch01_0853.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria regarding the crediting of manual operator actions in Diversity and Defense-in-Depth (D3) analyses, as outlined in SRP Appendix 18-A, Rev 0. It confirms that NuScale’s approach aligns with the updated review criteria for all phases of D3 analysis, superseding previous guidance, while noting that certain aspects of probabilistic risk assessment remain the responsibility of the COL applicant.

nuscale_ch01_0066.title: Material Referenced
nuscale_ch01_0066.summary: This section lists the topical and technical reports referenced in the NuScale Power Plant Design Certification Application, as detailed in Tables 1.6-1 and 1.6-2. These reports cover various aspects such as quality assurance, risk significance, safety classification, accident analysis, and methodology qualifications, and are incorporated by reference into the Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR).

nuscale_ch01_0505.title: DSRS 11.5, Rev 0: Process and Effluent Radiological Monitoring Instrumentation and Sampling SystemsII.2 Instrumentation and Monitoring Equipment and Sampling and Analysis of Radioactive Waste Process Systems (Including Appendix 11.5A)
nuscale_ch01_0505.summary: This section reviews NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for process and effluent radiological monitoring instrumentation and sampling systems. It notes that while the design partially meets the acceptance criteria for instrumentation, monitoring, and sampling of radioactive waste process systems, some aspects of the regulatory guidance are not fully addressed and administrative/procedural controls are the responsibility of the COL applicant. Specific regulatory guides and areas of partial conformance are referenced throughout.

nuscale_ch01_0852.title: SRP Appendix 18-A, Rev 0: Crediting Manual Operator Actions in Diversity and Defense-in-Depth (D3) AnalysesC.1.B Review Criteria for Phase 1 (Analysis)
nuscale_ch01_0852.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for crediting manual operator actions in Diversity and Defense-in-Depth (D3) analyses, as outlined in SRP Appendix 18-A, Rev 0. It confirms that NuScale meets the review criteria for all phases of D3 analysis, superseding previous guidance, while noting that certain aspects of probabilistic risk assessment (PRA) remain the responsibility of the COL applicant.

nuscale_ch01_0747.title: SRP 15.4.6, Rev 2: Inadvertent Decrease in Boron Concentration in the Reactor Coolant System (PWR)II.1 Reactor Coolant and Main Steam System Pressures
nuscale_ch01_0747.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with SRP 15.4.6, Rev 2, regarding inadvertent decreases in boron concentration in the reactor coolant system for PWRs. It states that NuScale’s design meets the relevant regulatory criteria for reactor coolant and main steam system pressures, fuel cladding integrity, incident frequency, and analysis methods, while noting that operator action is not required for mitigation and some guidance is not applicable due to design differences (e.g., absence of reactor coolant pumps).

nuscale_ch01_0110.title: Figure 1.7-1b: Electrical Symbols
nuscale_ch01_0110.summary: This section provides a legend of electrical symbols and abbreviations used in NuScale’s Final Safety Analysis Report drawings, including representations for switches, relays, transformers, and logic gates. It also explains the meaning of indicator light colors, logic gate functions, and common abbreviations relevant to electrical and control systems.

nuscale_ch01_0115.title: Figure 1.7-3d: Piping and Instrumentation Diagram Legends
nuscale_ch01_0115.summary: This section provides the legend for interpreting Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams (P&IDs) in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report. It details the identification letters used for instrumentation, explaining the meaning of each letter and their combinations to represent measured variables, functions, and device types. The table helps users understand how to read and interpret instrument codes and symbols on the diagrams.

nuscale_ch01_0708.title: DSRS 15.2.1-15.2.5, Rev 0: Loss of External Load; Turbine Trip; Loss of Condenser Vacuum; Closure of Main Steam Isolation Valve; and Steam Pressure Regulator Failure (Closed)II.2.D Instrument Setpoints - Impact on Plant Response
nuscale_ch01_0708.summary: The section confirms that the NuScale design meets regulatory criteria for events such as loss of external load, turbine trip, loss of condenser vacuum, main steam isolation valve closure, and steam pressure regulator failure (closed), even though NuScale does not have a steam pressure regulator. For instrument setpoints and their impact on plant response, as well as other safety-related criteria, the design is shown to conform with no exceptions or additional comments.

nuscale_ch01_0748.title: SRP 15.4.6, Rev 2: Inadvertent Decrease in Boron Concentration in the Reactor Coolant System (PWR)II.2 Fuel Cladding Integrity
nuscale_ch01_0748.summary: The section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria regarding inadvertent decreases in boron concentration in the reactor coolant system, specifically addressing fuel cladding integrity. It states that NuScale meets the relevant requirements, noting that Critical Heat Flux (CHF) is the appropriate term for their design, and that operator action is not required to mitigate such events. The analysis methods and assumptions also conform to the applicable standards.

nuscale_ch01_0038.title: Main Control Room
nuscale_ch01_0038.summary: The Main Control Room (MCR) is located below grade in the Control Room Building (CRB) and includes various support spaces such as offices, conference rooms, and equipment rooms. It houses control panels for all installed NuScale Power Modules (NPMs), allowing reactor operators to monitor and control multiple NPMs using digital control systems that ensure independence between safety and nonsafety systems. The MCR provides comprehensive monitoring, alarms, and controls for plant operations, with additional display stations for shared systems and diagnostics.

nuscale_ch01_0224.title: DSRS 7.2.6, Rev 0: Derivation of System InputsAll Various
nuscale_ch01_0224.summary: This section summarizes NuScale's conformance with various regulatory guides and standards related to digital instrumentation and control (I&C) systems, noting areas of full or partial compliance. Specifically, for DSRS 7.2.6, Rev 0, it states that the derivation of system inputs conforms to requirements, and there are no specific DSRS acceptance criteria for this item.

nuscale_ch01_0069.title: Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams
nuscale_ch01_0069.summary: This section describes the use of simplified instrumentation, control, electrical, and mechanical drawings in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report to visually clarify design information alongside written text. It lists the relevant diagrams and legends for both electrical/I&C and piping/instrumentation systems, and notes that site-specific diagrams and legends will be provided by COL applicants referencing the NuScale design certification.

nuscale_ch01_0225.title: DSRS 7.2.7, Rev 0: Setpoints II.1 Conformance to RG 1.105
nuscale_ch01_0225.summary: This section summarizes the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report’s conformance with various regulatory guides and standards related to digital instrumentation and control (I&C) systems. It details which criteria are fully or partially met for equipment qualification, reliability, protective actions, interlocks, and system input derivation, referencing specific regulatory guides and IEEE standards. Where partial conformance exists, further details are provided in referenced tables.

nuscale_ch01_0687.title: DSRS 15.1.5, Rev 0: Steam System Piping Failures Inside and Outside of ContainmentII.9 AssumptionsPostulated Failure of Seismic Main Steam Line
nuscale_ch01_0687.summary: This section outlines the assumptions related to postulated failures of both seismic and non-seismic main steam lines in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report, confirming conformance with DSRS 15.1.5 requirements. It notes that operator action is not required to mitigate steam line break consequences, and clarifies that radiological analysis requirements are superseded by SRP Section 15.0.3 for design certification applications, while nonradiological analysis provisions remain applicable.
